   Rows 4-7, Col 7:   T29 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   Rows 8-11, Col 0:  T2  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 8-11, Col 1:  T6  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 8-11, Col 7:  T30 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   Rows 12-15, Col 0: T3  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 12-15, Col 1: T7  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 12-15, Col 7: T31 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are assigned along the N (column) dimension first, then the K (row) dimension:
   - Column 0 (N=0): T0 (rows 0-3), T1 (rows 4-7), T2 (rows 8-11), T3 (rows 12-15)
   - Column 1 (N=1): T4 (rows 0-3), T5 (rows 4-7), T6 (rows 8-11), T7 (rows 12-15)
   - Column 2 (N=2): T8 (rows 0-3), T9 (rows 4-7), T10 (rows 8-11), T11 (rows 12-15)  [implied by pattern]
   - ...continuing...
   - Column 7 (N=7): T28 (rows 0-3), T29 (rows 4-7), T30 (rows 8-11), T31 (rows 12-15)

   Thread numbering formula: Thread = (col * 4) + (row_group)
   where row_group = 0 for rows 0-3, 1 for rows 4-7, 2 for rows 8-11, 3 for rows 12-15

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   Each thread holds 4 register elements: b0, b1, b2, b3.
   Since the data type is .u8 or .s8 (8-bit values), four 8-bit values are packed into each 32-bit register.
   Each thread covers 4 consecutive rows (K dimension) within its assigned column (N dimension).
   b0 corresponds to the first row of the group, b1 to the second, b2 to the third, b3 to the fourth.
   All 4 elements (b0-b3) are packed into a single 32-bit register (4 x 8-bit = 32 bits).

6. ANNOTATION
   The figure label at the bottom reads: %laneid:{fragment}
   The arrow in column 2 indicates the pattern continues from col 0-1 through col 7 following the same thread assignment structure across all 32 lanes (T0-T31).


  Figure 57 MMA .m8n8k16 fragment layout for matrix B with `.u8`/`.s8` type

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =  (threadID_in_group * 4) + i         for bi    where i = {0,..,3}

  col =    groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing of two `.s32` registers. | c0, c1 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 58](#mma-8816-c-i8).

   - Columns (0 through 7) represent the 8 columns of the accumulator matrix

   - Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

3. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each thread holds 2 elements: {c0, c1}
   - Since the data type is .s32, each element occupies one full 32-bit register
   - Each thread therefore uses 2 registers for this fragment

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Each row of the 8x8 accumulator matrix is covered by 4 threads
   - Each of those 4 threads covers 2 consecutive columns (a column pair)
   - Row 0 → Threads T0, T1, T2, T3
   - Row 1 → Threads T4, T5, T6, T7
   - Row 2 → Threads T8, T9, T10, T11
   - Row 3 → Threads T12, T13, T14, T15
   - Row 4 → Threads T16, T17, T18, T19
   - Row 5 → Threads T20, T21, T22, T23
   - Row 6 → Threads T24, T25, T26, T27
   - Row 7 → Threads T28, T29, T30, T31
   - General rule: For matrix row R, threads T(4R) through T(4R+3) hold that row
   - Within each row, thread T(4R+0) holds columns 0-1, T(4R+1) holds columns 2-3, T(4R+2) holds columns 4-5, T(4R+3) holds columns 6-7

   - c0 corresponds to the lower-indexed column of the pair (e.g., column 0 for T0, column 2 for T1)
   - c1 corresponds to the higher-indexed column of the pair (e.g., column 1 for T0, column 3 for T1)
   - Each of c0 and c1 is one 32-bit .s32 register (no packing; one element per register)
   - Total registers per thread for this fragment: 2

##### 9.7.14.5.4. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m8n8k32`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-8832)

A warp executing `mma.m8n8k32` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m8n8k32`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing a single `.b32` register, containing eight `.s4` or `.u4` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 59](#mma-8832-a-i4).

  



[Figure: mma-8832-A-i4]

OVERVIEW
Matrix: A
Dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=32
Data type: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit) or .s4 (signed 4-bit)
Total threads involved: 32 (T0 through T31)
Notation format: %laneid:{fragments}

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has rows representing matrix rows (0 through 7) and columns representing the K-dimension column ranges (0..7, 8..15, 16..23, 24..31). Each cell contains a thread ID and its associated register fragments.

COLUMN GROUPINGS (K-dimension segments)
Col 0..7    = K indices 0 through 7
Col 8..15   = K indices 8 through 15
Col 16..23  = K indices 16 through 23
Col 24..31  = K indices 24 through 31

  Col 8..15:  T1  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}

  Col 24..31: T3  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}

  Col 24..31: T7  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}

  (implied continuation of pattern with arrow indicating progression)

Rows 3..6:
  (implied by the ".." notation and arrows, continuing the pattern)

  Col 16..23: T30 holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 24..31: T31 holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}

GROUPING PATTERN
Each matrix row is handled by 4 consecutive threads (groups of 4).
Row 0: T0-T3

Row 2: T8-T11
Row 3: T12-T15
Row 4: T16-T19
Row 5: T20-T23
Row 6: T24-T27

Each thread holds 8 elements labeled a0 through a7.

Elements a0..a7 represent the 8 K-dimension values assigned to that thread within its column segment.

  row = (threadID_in_group * 8) + i         for bi   where i = {0,..,7}

  col = groupID
  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.5. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m8n8k128`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-88128)

A warp executing `mma.m8n8k128` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m8n8k128`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing a single `.b32` register, containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, … a30, a31 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 62](#mma-88128-a).

  



[Figure: mma-88128-A]
MMA .m8n8k128 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .b1 type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m8n8k128
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary / boolean)
   Full matrix dimensions: 8 rows x 128 columns (M=8, N=8, K=128)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (R): 0 through 7 — these represent the 8 rows of matrix A
   Columns (C): 0 through 127 — these represent the 128 columns of matrix A
   Column groupings: 0-31, 32-63, 64-95, 96-127 (four groups of 32 columns each)
   Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per row and column group)

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-31:   T0  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 32-63:  T1  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 64-95:  T2  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 96-127: T3  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0-31:   T4  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 32-63:  T5  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 64-95:  T6  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 96-127: T7  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}

   Rows 2 through 6 (indicated by arrows showing continuation of the same pattern):
     Each row uses the next group of 4 threads in sequence, following the same 4-thread-per-row pattern.
     Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11 (implied by pattern)
     Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
     Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
     Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
     Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-31:   T28 holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 32-63:  T29 holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 64-95:  T30 holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 96-127: T31 holds {a0, a1, .. a31}

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   Each matrix row is covered by exactly 4 consecutive threads.
   Each thread covers exactly 32 consecutive columns of its assigned row.
   Thread assignment: Thread T(4*row + col_group) covers row R and column group col_group*32 to col_group*32+31.
   Specifically: T(4r + c) where r = row index (0-7), c = column group index (0-3).

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds registers labeled a0 through a31 (32 register elements).
   Since the data type is .b1 (1-bit), 32 bits are packed into each 32-bit register.
   Each thread holds 32 bits total, covering 32 consecutive 1-bit elements from its assigned 32-column segment.
   The notation {a0, a1, .. a31} indicates 32 individual 1-bit values packed into what is effectively one 32-bit register (or equivalently, 32 bits packed across registers).

6. ADDRESSING NOTE
   The figure notes: %laneid:{fragments}
   This confirms that the lane ID (0-31 within the warp) directly indexes which thread holds which fragment, following the pattern described above.

     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2 (matrix row 2):
     (Indicated by arrow spanning the row — same pattern continues with threads T8-T11, implied by the arrow showing continuation of the pattern)

   Rows 3-6 (matrix rows 3-6):
     Indicated by ".." — pattern continues with threads T12-T27 following the same structure

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}

   - 4 threads per matrix row, each thread covers 2 consecutive columns
   - 8 rows total, 4 threads per row = 32 threads total (T0 through T31)

   - Each thread holds 2 accumulator elements: c0 and c1
   - Since the data type is .s32, each element is one full 32-bit register
   - Therefore each thread holds 2 separate 32-bit registers: one for c0, one for c1

   T1     | 0          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T2     | 0          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T3     | 0          | 6, 7        | c0, c1
   T4     | 1          | 0, 1        | c0, c1
   T5     | 1          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T6     | 1          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T7     | 1          | 6, 7        | c0, c1
   ...    | ...        | ...         | ...
   T28    | 7          | 0, 1        | c0, c1
   T29    | 7          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T30    | 7          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T31    | 7          | 6, 7        | c0, c1

MMA .m8n8k128 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   - Instruction: mma .m8n8k128
   - Matrix: C/D (Accumulator)
   - Data type: .s32 (32-bit signed integer)
   - Matrix dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=128

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows (labeled Row\Col): 0, 1, 2, .., 7  (8 rows total, representing matrix rows 0-7)
   - Columns: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7  (8 columns total, grouped into pairs: [0,1], [2,3], [4,5], [6,7])
   - Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per row)

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T1:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T2:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Columns 0-1: T4:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T5:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2 (matrix row 2):
     (Indicated by arrow spanning the row — same pattern continues with threads T8-T11, implied by the arrow showing continuation of the pattern)

   Rows 3-6 (matrix rows 3-6):
     Indicated by ".." — pattern continues with threads T12-T27 following the same structure

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   - 4 threads per matrix row, each thread covers 2 consecutive columns
   - 8 rows total, 4 threads per row = 32 threads total (T0 through T31)
   - Thread assignment per row:
       Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
       Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
       Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11  (implied)
       Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
       Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
       Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
       Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
       Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31
   - Each thread Tn in row r covers columns [2*(n mod 4)*2 .. 2*(n mod 4)*2+1]

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each thread holds 2 accumulator elements: c0 and c1
   - Since the data type is .s32, each element is one full 32-bit register
   - Therefore each thread holds 2 separate 32-bit registers: one for c0, one for c1
   - No sub-register packing (unlike f16 which packs two values per register)
   - c0 corresponds to the first (lower-index) column of the thread's column pair
   - c1 corresponds to the second (higher-index) column of the thread's column pair

6. SUMMARY TABLE
   Thread | Matrix Row | Matrix Cols | Registers
   T0     | 0          | 0, 1        | c0, c1
   T1     | 0          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T2     | 0          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T3     | 0          | 6, 7        | c0, c1
   T4     | 1          | 0, 1        | c0, c1
   T5     | 1          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T6     | 1          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T7     | 1          | 6, 7        | c0, c1
   ...    | ...        | ...         | ...
   T28    | 7          | 0, 1        | c0, c1
   T29    | 7          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T30    | 7          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T31    | 7          | 6, 7        | c0, c1


  Figure 64 MMA .m8n8k128 fragment layout for accumulator matrix C/D with `.s32` type

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + i    for ci where i = {0, 1}
  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.6. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k4`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-1684)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k4` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k4`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  + `.tf32`:

    | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    | --- | --- | --- |
    | `.tf32` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, containing two `.tf32` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1 |

    The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 65](#mma-1684-a-tf32).

    



[Figure: mma-1684-A]
MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k4
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix A logical dimensions: 16 rows x 4 columns (M=16, K=4)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled by matrix row index (0-15) and columns labeled 0-3 (Col 0 through Col 3).
   Columns 0-3 represent the 4 column positions (K=4 dimension) of matrix A.
   The notation is: Tx:ay where x = thread lane ID, y = register fragment index (a0 or a1).

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete, per row)

   UPPER HALF (matrix rows 0-7, register fragment a0):

   Row 0:  Col0=T0:a0,  Col1=T1:a0,  Col2=T2:a0,  Col3=T3:a0
   Row 1:  Col0=T4:a0,  Col1=T5:a0,  Col2=T6:a0,  Col3=T7:a0
   Row 2:  (pattern continues with arrows indicating same thread/register assignment pattern)
   Rows 2-6: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 2-6 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 7:  Col0=T28:a0, Col1=T29:a0, Col2=T30:a0, Col3=T31:a0

   LOWER HALF (matrix rows 8-15, register fragment a1):

   Row 8:  Col0=T0:a1,  Col1=T1:a1,  Col2=T2:a1,  Col3=T3:a1
   Row 9:  Col0=T4:a1,  Col1=T5:a1,  Col2=T6:a1,  Col3=T7:a1
   Rows 10-14: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 10-14 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 15: Col0=T28:a1, Col1=T29:a1, Col2=T30:a1, Col3=T31:a1

4. DATA TYPE
   .f64 — each element is a 64-bit double precision float.
   Each element occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX representation).

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
   Each thread holds exactly 2 fragments: a0 and a1.
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per matrix row:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 0  (fragment a0)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 1  (fragment a0)
     T8-T11  -> matrix row 2  (fragment a0)
     T12-T15 -> matrix row 3  (fragment a0)
     T16-T19 -> matrix row 4  (fragment a0)
     T20-T23 -> matrix row 5  (fragment a0)
     T24-T27 -> matrix row 6  (fragment a0)
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 7  (fragment a0)
   The same thread grouping repeats for rows 8-15 using fragment a1:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 8  (fragment a1)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 9  (fragment a1)
     ...
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 15 (fragment a1)
   Within each group of 4 threads, each thread holds one of the 4 column elements (columns 0-3).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each matrix element is stored in one dedicated 64-bit register fragment.
   There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).
   Each thread holds 2 registers total for matrix A: a0 (one f64 element from the upper 8 rows) and a1 (one f64 element from the lower 8 rows).

   Index label: %laneid:{fragments} — the lane ID determines which column and row the thread's element corresponds to.

MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   Instruction: mma.m16n8k4
   Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=4
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled 0-15 (matrix rows, with intermediate rows implied by arrows and ".." notation) and columns labeled 0-7 (matrix columns).
   Columns are paired: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7} — each pair is covered by one thread entry.
   Threads T0 through T31 (32 threads total) are used.

3. REGISTER ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD

   UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 0-7), registers c0 and c1:

   Row 0,  Cols 0-1:  T0:  {c0, c1}
   Row 0,  Cols 2-3:  T1:  {c0, c1}
   Row 0,  Cols 4-5:  T2:  {c0, c1}
   Row 0,  Cols 6-7:  T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1,  Cols 0-1:  T4:  {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  Cols 2-3:  T5:  {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  Cols 4-5:  T6:  {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  Cols 6-7:  T7:  {c0, c1}

   Rows 2-6 follow the same pattern (implied by arrows and ".." notation), continuing with T8-T27 in groups of 4 threads per row, each thread holding {c0, c1}.

   Row 7,  Cols 0-1:  T28: {c0, c1}
   Row 7,  Cols 2-3:  T29: {c0, c1}
   Row 7,  Cols 4-5:  T30: {c0, c1}
   Row 7,  Cols 6-7:  T31: {c0, c1}

   LOWER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 8-15), registers c2 and c3:

   Row 8,  Cols 0-1:  T0:  {c2, c3}
   Row 8,  Cols 2-3:  T1:  {c2, c3}
   Row 8,  Cols 4-5:  T2:  {c2, c3}
   Row 8,  Cols 6-7:  T3:  {c2, c3}

   Row 9,  Cols 0-1:  T4:  {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  Cols 2-3:  T5:  {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  Cols 4-5:  T6:  {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  Cols 6-7:  T7:  {c2, c3}

   Rows 10-14 follow the same pattern (implied by arrows and ".." notation), continuing with T8-T27 in groups of 4 threads per row, each thread holding {c2, c3}.

   Row 15, Cols 0-1:  T28: {c2, c3}
   Row 15, Cols 2-3:  T29: {c2, c3}
   Row 15, Cols 4-5:  T30: {c2, c3}
   Row 15, Cols 6-7:  T31: {c2, c3}

4. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Each thread covers exactly 2 columns of the matrix at a given row.
   Threads T0-T3 handle row 0 (cols 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively).
   Threads T4-T7 handle row 1.
   Each subsequent group of 4 threads (T8-T11, T12-T15, T16-T19, T20-T23, T24-T27) handles rows 2-6 respectively.
   Threads T28-T31 handle row 7.
   The same thread assignments repeat for rows 8-15, but using registers c2 and c3 instead of c0 and c1.
   Each thread T0-T31 therefore owns 4 registers total: c0, c1 (for the upper 8 rows) and c2, c3 (for the lower 8 rows).

5. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Each element is .f64 (64-bit double precision).
   Each register c0, c1, c2, c3 holds one f64 value (one matrix element).
   No packing of multiple values per register — each f64 element occupies its own register (or register pair in 32-bit register file terms, i.e., each f64 uses two 32-bit registers).
   The notation {c0, c1} means two separate f64 registers, each holding one matrix element.

6. LABEL AT BOTTOM
   %laneid:{fragments} — indicating the lane ID maps to the fragment registers as described above.

    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4
    >
    > row =      groupID                            for c0 and c1
    >          groupID + 8                          for c2 and c3
    >
    > col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci   where i = {0,..,3}
    > ```

##### 9.7.14.5.7. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k8`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-1688)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k8` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k8`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  + `.f16` and `.bf16` :

    > | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.f16` / `.bf16` | A vector expression containing two `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` / `.bf16` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 71](#mma-1688-a-f16).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-1688-A-f16]
MATRIX MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

INSTRUCTION: MMA .m16n8k8
MATRIX: A (input matrix)
DATA TYPE: .f16 or .bf16
MATRIX DIMENSIONS: M=16, N=8, K=8

GRID STRUCTURE
--------------
The table has Row\Col headers.
Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 15).
Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7).
Only columns 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 are shown, but thread assignments only appear in columns 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 (pairs).

Specifically, thread assignments appear at column positions 0, 2, 4, 6 (each spanning two columns visually), corresponding to 4 thread groups per row.

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING
--------------------------

UPPER HALF OF MATRIX A (rows 0 through 7):

Row 0:  col 0-1: T0:{a0, a1}   col 2-3: T1:{a0, a1}   col 4-5: T2:{a0, a1}   col 6-7: T3:{a0, a1}
Row 1:  col 0-1: T4:{a0, a1}   col 2-3: T5:{a0, a1}   col 4-5: T6:{a0, a1}   col 6-7: T7:{a0, a1}
Row 2:  (continuation pattern indicated by arrows, threads T8-T11 implied)
...
Row 7:  col 0-1: T28:{a0, a1}  col 2-3: T29:{a0, a1}  col 4-5: T30:{a0, a1}  col 6-7: T31:{a0, a1}

LOWER HALF OF MATRIX A (rows 8 through 15):

Row 8:  col 0-1: T0:{a2, a3}   col 2-3: T1:{a2, a3}   col 4-5: T2:{a2, a3}   col 6-7: T3:{a2, a3}
Row 9:  col 0-1: T4:{a2, a3}   col 2-3: T5:{a2, a3}   col 4-5: T6:{a2, a3}   col 6-7: T7:{a2, a3}
Row 10: (continuation pattern indicated by arrows, threads T8-T11 implied)
...
Row 15: col 0-1: T28:{a2, a3}  col 2-3: T29:{a2, a3}  col 4-5: T30:{a2, a3}  col 6-7: T31:{a2, a3}

GROUPING PATTERN
----------------
Rows 0-7 map to threads T0 through T31 (one thread per pair of rows, 4 threads per row across columns 0-7).
Each thread covers 2 consecutive columns.
Rows 0-1: T0-T7 (T0,T1,T2,T3 on row 0; T4,T5,T6,T7 on row 1)
Rows 2-3: T8-T15 (implied by arrows)
Rows 4-5: T16-T23 (implied by arrows)
Rows 6-7: T24-T31 (T28,T29,T30,T31 on row 7 explicitly shown)

The same thread assignment repeats for rows 8-15, but with different register elements {a2, a3} instead of {a0, a1}.

REGISTER PACKING
----------------
Each thread holds 2 register fragments for the upper half (rows 0-7): a0 and a1, packed as {a0, a1} in one 32-bit register (two f16 or bf16 values packed into a single 32-bit register).
Each thread holds 2 register fragments for the lower half (rows 8-15): a2 and a3, packed as {a2, a3} in one 32-bit register.
Total per thread: 4 elements (a0, a1, a2, a3) stored in 2 registers of 32 bits each.

LABEL AT BOTTOM
---------------
%laneid:{fragments}
This indicates the notation used: lane ID maps to the listed fragment elements.

   Rows 0-7 form the upper half; rows 8-15 form the lower half.
   Columns 0-3 form the left half; columns 4-7 form the right half.
   All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
   Each thread holds 4 register elements: a0, a1, a2, a3.

3. COMPLETE ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-TO-REGISTER MAPPING

   UPPER-LEFT QUADRANT (rows 0-7, columns 0-3): register element a0
     Row 0:  col 0 = T0:a0,  col 1 = T1:a0,  col 2 = T2:a0,  col 3 = T3:a0
     Row 1:  col 0 = T4:a0,  col 1 = T5:a0,  col 2 = T6:a0,  col 3 = T7:a0
     Rows 2-6: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 7:  col 0 = T28:a0, col 1 = T29:a0, col 2 = T30:a0, col 3 = T31:a0

   UPPER-RIGHT QUADRANT (rows 0-7, columns 4-7): register element a2
     Row 0:  col 4 = T0:a2,  col 5 = T1:a2,  col 6 = T2:a2,  col 7 = T3:a2
     Row 1:  col 4 = T4:a2,  col 5 = T5:a2,  col 6 = T6:a2,  col 7 = T7:a2
     Rows 2-6: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 7:  col 4 = T28:a2, col 5 = T29:a2, col 6 = T30:a2, col 7 = T31:a2

   LOWER-LEFT QUADRANT (rows 8-15, columns 0-3): register element a1
     Row 8:  col 0 = T0:a1,  col 1 = T1:a1,  col 2 = T2:a1,  col 3 = T3:a1
     Row 9:  col 0 = T4:a1,  col 1 = T5:a1,  col 2 = T6:a1,  col 3 = T7:a1
     Rows 10-14: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 15: col 0 = T28:a1, col 1 = T29:a1, col 2 = T30:a1, col 3 = T31:a1

   LOWER-RIGHT QUADRANT (rows 8-15, columns 4-7): register element a3
     Row 8:  col 4 = T0:a3,  col 5 = T1:a3,  col 6 = T2:a3,  col 7 = T3:a3
     Row 9:  col 4 = T4:a3,  col 5 = T5:a3,  col 6 = T6:a3,  col 7 = T7:a3

     Row 15: col 4 = T28:a3, col 5 = T29:a3, col 6 = T30:a3, col 7 = T31:a3

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per row:
     Threads T0-T3   cover row 0 (and rows 0,8 for a0/a1/a2/a3)
     Threads T4-T7   cover row 1 (and rows 1,9)
     Threads T8-T11  cover rows 2,10 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T12-T15 cover rows 3,11 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T16-T19 cover rows 4,12 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T20-T23 cover rows 5,13 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T24-T27 cover rows 6,14 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T28-T31 cover row 7 (and rows 7,15)
   Each group of 4 threads covers one matrix row across 4 columns in each half.

5. REGISTER ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD
   Each thread Tn holds exactly 4 register elements:
     a0: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread
     a1: covers the element in rows 8-15, columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread
     a2: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 4-7 corresponding to that thread
     a3: covers the element in rows 8-15, columns 4-7 corresponding to that thread

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision).
   Each register element (a0, a1, a2, a3) holds exactly ONE f64 value.
   Since f64 is 64 bits, each element occupies two 32-bit registers (a register pair) in PTX.
   No packing of multiple values per register element occurs (unlike f16 which packs two values).

   The figure notes "%laneid:{fragments}" at the bottom, indicating the mapping is indexed by lane ID within the warp.

     Row 8:  col 4 = T0:a3,  col 5 = T1:a3,  col 6 = T2:a3,  col 7 = T3:a3
     Row 9:  col 4 = T4:a3,  col 5 = T5:a3,  col 6 = T6:a3,  col 7 = T7:a3
     Rows 10-14: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 15: col 4 = T28:a3, col 5 = T29:a3, col 6 = T30:a3, col 7 = T31:a3

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per row:
     Threads T0-T3   cover row 0 (and rows 0,8 for a0/a1/a2/a3)
     Threads T4-T7   cover row 1 (and rows 1,9)
     Threads T8-T11  cover rows 2,10 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T12-T15 cover rows 3,11 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T16-T19 cover rows 4,12 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T20-T23 cover rows 5,13 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T24-T27 cover rows 6,14 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T28-T31 cover row 7 (and rows 7,15)
   Each group of 4 threads covers one matrix row across 4 columns in each half.

5. REGISTER ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD
   Each thread Tn holds exactly 4 register elements:
     a0: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread
     a1: covers the element in rows 8-15, columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread
     a2: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 4-7 corresponding to that thread
     a3: covers the element in rows 8-15, columns 4-7 corresponding to that thread

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision).
   Each register element (a0, a1, a2, a3) holds exactly ONE f64 value.
   Since f64 is 64 bits, each element occupies two 32-bit registers (a register pair) in PTX.
   No packing of multiple values per register element occurs (unlike f16 which packs two values).

7. LANE ID NOTE
   The figure notes "%laneid:{fragments}" at the bottom, indicating the mapping is indexed by lane ID within the warp.


    Figure 73 MMA .m16n8k8 fragment layout for matrix A with `.f64` type.

    The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

    ```
    groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

    row =      groupID            for a0 and a2
               groupID + 8        for a1 and a3

    col =  threadID_in_group       for a0 and a1
           threadID_in_group + 4   for a2 and a3
    ```
* Multiplicand B:

  + `.f16` and `.bf16` :

    > | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.f16` / `.bf16` | A vector expression containing a single `.f16x2` register, containing two `.f16` / `.bf16` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 74](#mma-1688-b-f16).

    >

MMA .m16n8k8 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f16/.bf16 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m16n8k8
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=8
   Data types: .f16 or .bf16

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table shows:
   Rows (labeled "Row\Column"): represent pairs of matrix rows (0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7), covering 8 rows total (K=8 dimension)
   Columns: 0, 1, 2, .. , 7 — representing 8 columns (N=8 dimension)
   Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

   Row pair 0-1 (matrix rows 0 and 1):
     Column 0: T0  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 1: T4  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 2: (continues pattern, arrow indicates continuation)
     ...
     Column 7: T28 holds {b0, b1}

   Row pair 2-3 (matrix rows 2 and 3):
     Column 0: T1  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 1: T5  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 2: (continues pattern)
     ...
     Column 7: T29 holds {b0, b1}

   Row pair 4-5 (matrix rows 4 and 5):
     Column 0: T2  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 1: T6  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 2: (continues pattern)
     ...
     Column 7: T30 holds {b0, b1}

   Row pair 6-7 (matrix rows 6 and 7):
     Column 0: T3  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 1: T7  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 2: (continues pattern)
     ...
     Column 7: T31 holds {b0, b1}

4. DATA TYPE
   .f16 or .bf16 (both supported as indicated in the caption)

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Threads are grouped in blocks of 4 consecutive threads per row-pair group:
     T0-T3:   cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, all at column 0
     T4-T7:   cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, at column 1
     T8-T11:  column 2 (implied by arrow continuation)
     T12-T15: column 3
     T16-T19: column 4
     T20-T23: column 5
     T24-T27: column 6
     T28-T31: cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, at column 7

   General rule: Thread T(n) covers:
     Row pair = n mod 4 (i.e., row pair index 0,1,2,3 corresponding to rows 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7)
     Column = floor(n / 4)

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds exactly 2 register elements: b0 and b1
   These are packed as two f16 (or bf16) values within a single 32-bit register
   b0 occupies the lower 16 bits of the register
   b1 occupies the upper 16 bits of the register
   Each thread thus holds one 32-bit register containing two consecutive elements from its assigned matrix position

   The notation %laneid:{fragments} at the bottom confirms the mapping is indexed by lane ID within the warp.

MMA .m16n8k8 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k8
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix B logical dimensions: K x N = 8 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has:
   Rows (labeled 0 through 7): represent the K dimension (8 rows of matrix B)
   Columns (labeled 0, 1, 2, .., 7): represent the N dimension (8 columns of matrix B)
   The table is divided into two horizontal sections:
     Upper section: rows 0-3 (K rows 0 through 3)
     Lower section: rows 4-7 (K rows 4 through 7)

3. ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-TO-REGISTER MAPPING

   Upper section (rows 0-3), register b0:
     Row 0, Column 0: T0:b0
     Row 0, Column 1: T4:b0
     Row 0, Column 7: T28:b0
     Row 1, Column 0: T1:b0
     Row 1, Column 1: T5:b0
     Row 1, Column 7: T29:b0
     Row 2, Column 0: T2:b0
     Row 2, Column 1: T6:b0
     Row 2, Column 7: T30:b0
     Row 3, Column 0: T3:b0
     Row 3, Column 1: T7:b0
     Row 3, Column 7: T31:b0

   Lower section (rows 4-7), register b1:
     Row 4, Column 0: T0:b1
     Row 4, Column 1: T4:b1
     Row 4, Column 7: T28:b1
     Row 5, Column 0: T1:b1
     Row 5, Column 1: T5:b1
     Row 5, Column 7: T29:b1
     Row 6, Column 0: T2:b1
     Row 6, Column 1: T6:b1
     Row 6, Column 7: T30:b1
     Row 7, Column 0: T3:b1
     Row 7, Column 1: T7:b1
     Row 7, Column 7: T31:b1

   Columns 2 through 6 (indicated by ".." with diagonal arrows) follow the same pattern as shown for columns 0, 1, and 7, continuing the thread numbering pattern.

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per column group:
     Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)
     Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)
     Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11 (implied by pattern)
     Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
     Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
     Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
     Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
     Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)

   Each group of 4 threads (Tn, Tn+1, Tn+2, Tn+3) covers one column of matrix B across all 8 K-rows, using registers b0 (for K-rows 0-3) and b1 (for K-rows 4-7).

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds 2 registers for matrix B:
     b0: holds the element from K-rows 0-3 for that thread's assigned column
     b1: holds the element from K-rows 4-7 for that thread's assigned column
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each register b0 and b1 is a 64-bit register holding one double-precision value. There is no sub-register packing (one element per register).

6. NOTATION
   %laneid:{fragments} indicates that the thread lane ID determines which fragment (matrix element) each thread holds.
   The diagonal arrows in columns 2 and ".." indicate the pattern continues consistently for all intermediate columns following the same thread-numbering scheme.

##### 9.7.14.5.8. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k16` with floating point type](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16816-float)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k16` floating point types will compute an MMA operation of shape
`.m16n8k16`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  + `.f16` and `.bf16` :

    > | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.f16` / `.bf16` | A vector expression containing four `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` / `.bf16` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 79](#mma-16816-a-f16).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-16816-A-f16]
MATRIX MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

INSTRUCTION: mma .m16n8k16
MATRIX: A (input matrix)
DATA TYPE: .f16 or .bf16
DIMENSIONS: M=16, N=8, K=16

GRID INTERPRETATION
-------------------
Rows (R) = matrix rows 0 through 15 (the M=16 dimension)
Columns (C) = matrix columns 0 through 15 (the K=16 dimension)
The table is divided into two halves:
  Left half: columns 0-7
  Right half: columns 8-15

REGISTER PACKING
----------------
Two f16 (or bf16) values are packed into a single 32-bit register.
Each thread holds 4 registers total: {a0,a1}, {a2,a3}, {a4,a5}, {a6,a7}
- {a0,a1} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a0 and a1)
- {a2,a3} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a2 and a3)
- {a4,a5} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a4 and a5)
- {a6,a7} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a6 and a7)

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete table)
------------------------------------------
Format: Row, Column range -> Thread: {fragments}

ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 0-7 (left-top quadrant):
  Row 0, Cols 0-3: T0:{a0,a1}  T1:{a0,a1}  T2:{a0,a1}  T3:{a0,a1}
  Row 1, Cols 0-3: T4:{a0,a1}  T5:{a0,a1}  T6:{a0,a1}  T7:{a0,a1}
  Rows 2-6: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern repeating)
  Row 7, Cols 0-3: T28:{a0,a1} T29:{a0,a1} T30:{a0,a1} T31:{a0,a1}

ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 8-15 (right-top quadrant):
  Row 0, Cols 8-11:  T0:{a4,a5}  T1:{a4,a5}  T2:{a4,a5}  T3:{a4,a5}
  Row 1, Cols 8-11:  T4:{a4,a5}  T5:{a4,a5}  T6:{a4,a5}  T7:{a4,a5}
  Rows 2-6: continuation pattern
  Row 7, Cols 8-11:  T28:{a4,a5} T29:{a4,a5} T30:{a4,a5} T31:{a4,a5}

ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 0-7 (left-bottom quadrant):
  Row 8,  Cols 0-3: T0:{a2,a3}  T1:{a2,a3}  T2:{a2,a3}  T3:{a2,a3}
  Row 9,  Cols 0-3: T4:{a2,a3}  T5:{a2,a3}  T6:{a2,a3}  T7:{a2,a3}
  Rows 10-14: continuation pattern
  Row 15, Cols 0-3: T28:{a2,a3} T29:{a2,a3} T30:{a2,a3} T31:{a2,a3}

ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 8-15 (right-bottom quadrant):
  Row 8,  Cols 8-11:  T0:{a6,a7}  T1:{a6,a7}  T2:{a6,a7}  T3:{a6,a7}
  Row 9,  Cols 8-11:  T4:{a6,a7}  T5:{a6,a7}  T6:{a6,a7}  T7:{a6,a7}
  Rows 10-14: continuation pattern
  Row 15, Cols 8-11:  T28:{a6,a7} T29:{a6,a7} T30:{a6,a7} T31:{a6,a7}

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
-------------------------
Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads, each group covering one matrix row:
  Row 0:  T0,  T1,  T2,  T3   (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 1:  T4,  T5,  T6,  T7   (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 2:  T8,  T9,  T10, T11  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 3:  T12, T13, T14, T15  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 4:  T16, T17, T18, T19  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 5:  T20, T21, T22, T23  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 6:  T24, T25, T26, T27  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 7:  T28, T29, T30, T31  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 8:  T0,  T1,  T2,  T3   (cols 0-3 and 8-11) -- same threads as row 0, different registers
  Row 9:  T4,  T5,  T6,  T7
  ...
  Row 15: T28, T29, T30, T31

REGISTER ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD (all 32 threads):
  Each thread holds exactly 4 registers (8 f16 elements total):
  - Register containing {a0,a1}: covers matrix rows 0-7,  columns 0-7
  - Register containing {a2,a3}: covers matrix rows 8-15, columns 0-7
  - Register containing {a4,a5}: covers matrix rows 0-7,  columns 8-15
  - Register containing {a6,a7}: covers matrix rows 8-15, columns 8-15

NOTE: The column coverage per thread within each row spans 4 consecutive columns (e.g., cols 0-3 for T0-T3 in row 0). The exact column offset within the 4-column span is determined by the thread's position within its group of 4. Each thread covers exactly 1 column position in each of the four 4-column sub-blocks (cols 0-3, cols 4-7 implied by pattern, cols 8-11, cols 12-15 implied by pattern).

  Row 7, Cols 8-11:  T28:{a4,a5} T29:{a4,a5} T30:{a4,a5} T31:{a4,a5}

ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 0-7 (left-bottom quadrant):
  Row 8,  Cols 0-3: T0:{a2,a3}  T1:{a2,a3}  T2:{a2,a3}  T3:{a2,a3}
  Row 9,  Cols 0-3: T4:{a2,a3}  T5:{a2,a3}  T6:{a2,a3}  T7:{a2,a3}
  Rows 10-14: continuation pattern
  Row 15, Cols 0-3: T28:{a2,a3} T29:{a2,a3} T30:{a2,a3} T31:{a2,a3}

ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 8-15 (right-bottom quadrant):
  Row 8,  Cols 8-11:  T0:{a6,a7}  T1:{a6,a7}  T2:{a6,a7}  T3:{a6,a7}

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
-------------------------
Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads, each group covering one matrix row:
  Row 0:  T0,  T1,  T2,  T3   (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 1:  T4,  T5,  T6,  T7   (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 2:  T8,  T9,  T10, T11  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 3:  T12, T13, T14, T15  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 4:  T16, T17, T18, T19  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 5:  T20, T21, T22, T23  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 6:  T24, T25, T26, T27  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 7:  T28, T29, T30, T31  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 8:  T0,  T1,  T2,  T3   (cols 0-3 and 8-11) -- same threads as row 0, different registers
  Row 9:  T4,  T5,  T6,  T7
  ...
  Row 15: T28, T29, T30, T31

REGISTER ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD (all 32 threads):
  Each thread holds exactly 4 registers (8 f16 elements total):
  - Register containing {a0,a1}: covers matrix rows 0-7,  columns 0-7
  - Register containing {a2,a3}: covers matrix rows 8-15, columns 0-7
  - Register containing {a4,a5}: covers matrix rows 0-7,  columns 8-15
  - Register containing {a6,a7}: covers matrix rows 8-15, columns 8-15

NOTE: The column coverage per thread within each row spans 4 consecutive columns (e.g., cols 0-3 for T0-T3 in row 0). The exact column offset within the 4-column span is determined by the thread's position within its group of 4. Each thread covers exactly 1 column position in each of the four 4-column sub-blocks (cols 0-3, cols 4-7 implied by pattern, cols 8-11, cols 12-15 implied by pattern).

    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:
    >
    > ```
    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4
    >
    > row =      groupID            for ai where  0 <= i < 2 || 4 <= i < 6
    >           groupID + 8         Otherwise
    >
    > col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)          for ai where i <  4
    > (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1) + 8      for ai where i >= 4
    > ```

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY

Each thread Tn holds exactly 8 fragments: a0 through a7.
Thread group assignment per row: row r uses threads T(4r) through T(4r+3) for r=0..7 in both the upper (rows 0-7) and lower (rows 8-15) bands.

Thread Tn (where n = 4r + c, r=0..7, c=0..3):
- a0: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4),    cols 0-3 band
- a1: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4),    cols 0-3 band
- a2: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4)+4,  cols 4-7 band
- a3: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4)+4,  cols 4-7 band
- a4: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4)+8,  cols 8-11 band
- a5: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4)+8,  cols 8-11 band
- a6: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4)+12, cols 12-15 band
- a7: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4)+12, cols 12-15 band

REGISTER PACKING
Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each fragment (a0 through a7) occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX). There is no packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register). Each fragment corresponds to exactly one double-precision scalar value.

- The label at the bottom is: %laneid:{fragments}

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

The matrix B has K=16 rows and N=8 columns. The layout is divided into two horizontal bands:

UPPER BAND (Matrix rows 0-7):
- Column 0: Threads T0 through T3 (T0 at rows 0-1, T3 at rows 6-7)
  - T0 holds {b0, b1} (two elements packed in one register)
  - T1 (implied, rows 2-3): holds {b0, b1}
  - T2 (implied, rows 4-5): holds {b0, b1}
  - T3 holds {b0, b1} (rows 6-7)
- Column 1: Threads T4 through T7
  - T4 holds {b0, b1}
  - T5 (implied): holds {b0, b1}
  - T6 (implied): holds {b0, b1}
  - T7 holds {b0, b1}
- Columns 2 through 6 (implied, shown as ".."):  follow same pattern
- Column 7: Threads T28 through T31
  - T28 holds {b0, b1}
  - T29 (implied): holds {b0, b1}
  - T30 (implied): holds {b0, b1}
  - T31 holds {b0, b1}

LOWER BAND (Matrix rows 8-15):
- Column 0: Threads T0 through T3
  - T0 holds {b2, b3} (rows 8-9)
  - T1 (implied, rows 10-11): holds {b2, b3}
  - T2 (implied, rows 12-13): holds {b2, b3}
  - T3 holds {b2, b3} (rows 14-15)
- Column 1: Threads T4 through T7
  - T4 holds {b2, b3}
  - T5 (implied): holds {b2, b3}
  - T6 (implied): holds {b2, b3}
  - T7 holds {b2, b3}
- Columns 2 through 6 (implied): follow same pattern
- Column 7: Threads T28 through T31
  - T28 holds {b2, b3}
  - T29 (implied): holds {b2, b3}
  - T30 (implied): holds {b2, b3}
  - T31 holds {b2, b3}

REGISTER ELEMENT NAMING AND PACKING
- b0, b1, b2, b3 are the fragment register names for matrix B
- Each thread holds 4 elements total: b0, b1 (from upper band, rows 0-7) and b2, b3 (from lower band, rows 8-15)
- Elements are packed two per 32-bit register: {b0, b1} packed into one 32-bit register, {b2, b3} packed into one 32-bit register
- Each thread thus uses 2 registers total for matrix B
- For .f16 or .bf16: two 16-bit values are packed into each 32-bit register

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
- 32 threads total (T0-T31) cover all 8 columns of matrix B
- Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one column of the N=8 dimension
- Within each group of 4 threads, each thread covers 2 consecutive rows in each band (2 rows in rows 0-7, 2 rows in rows 8-15)
- Each thread owns exactly 2 elements per band (b0,b1 or b2,b3), for 4 elements total across both bands
- Column index maps to thread group: column c corresponds to threads T(4c) through T(4c+3)

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
Operation: MMA m16n8k16
Matrix: B (input matrix)
Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
Matrix B logical dimensions: 16 rows x 8 columns (K=16 rows, N=8 columns)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
The table has:
Rows (labeled 0-15): represent the 16 rows of matrix B (K dimension)
Columns (labeled 0, 1, 2, ..., 7): represent the 8 columns of matrix B (N dimension)
Entries: ThreadID:RegisterFragment notation (e.g., T0:B0 means Thread 0, register fragment B0)

3. COMPLETE THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

The table shows columns 0, 1, and 7 explicitly. Columns 2 through 6 are implied by the downward arrows (continuation pattern).

ROWS 0-3 (first group, register B0):
Row 0: Col 0 = T0:B0,  Col 1 = T4:B0,  Col 7 = T28:B0
Row 1: Col 0 = T1:B0,  Col 1 = T5:B0,  Col 7 = T29:B0
Row 2: Col 0 = T2:B0,  Col 1 = T6:B0,  Col 7 = T30:B0
Row 3: Col 0 = T3:B0,  Col 1 = T7:B0,  Col 7 = T31:B0

ROWS 4-7 (second group, register B1):
Row 4: Col 0 = T0:B1,  Col 1 = T4:B1,  Col 7 = T28:B1
Row 5: Col 0 = T1:B1,  Col 1 = T5:B1,  Col 7 = T29:B1
Row 6: Col 0 = T2:B1,  Col 1 = T6:B1,  Col 7 = T30:B1
Row 7: Col 0 = T3:B1,  Col 1 = T7:B1,  Col 7 = T31:B1

ROWS 8-11 (third group, register B2):
Row 8:  Col 0 = T0:B2,  Col 1 = T4:B2,  Col 7 = T28:B2
Row 9:  Col 0 = T1:B2,  Col 1 = T5:B2,  Col 7 = T29:B2
Row 10: Col 0 = T2:B2,  Col 1 = T6:B2,  Col 7 = T30:B2
Row 11: Col 0 = T3:B2,  Col 1 = T7:B2,  Col 7 = T31:B2

ROWS 12-15 (fourth group, register B3):
Row 12: Col 0 = T0:B3,  Col 1 = T4:B3,  Col 7 = T28:B3
Row 13: Col 0 = T1:B3,  Col 1 = T5:B3,  Col 7 = T29:B3
Row 14: Col 0 = T2:B3,  Col 1 = T6:B3,  Col 7 = T30:B3
Row 15: Col 0 = T3:B3,  Col 1 = T7:B3,  Col 7 = T31:B3

4. DATA TYPE
.f64 = 64-bit double precision floating point
Each register fragment (B0, B1, B2, B3) holds one f64 value (one 64-bit element), stored as a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX (since PTX registers are 32-bit, each f64 occupies two consecutive 32-bit registers).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS

Column grouping (N dimension, 8 columns total):
Columns 0-7 are distributed across threads T0-T31 in groups of 4 threads per column-pair.
Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, T8-T11, ..., T28-T31) covers one column of matrix B.
Specifically:
  Column 0: threads T0, T1, T2, T3 (rows 0,1,2,3 respectively within each row-group)
  Column 1: threads T4, T5, T6, T7
  Column 2: threads T8, T9, T10, T11
  Column 3: threads T12, T13, T14, T15
  Column 4: threads T16, T17, T18, T19
  Column 5: threads T20, T21, T22, T23
  Column 6: threads T24, T25, T26, T27
  Column 7: threads T28, T29, T30, T31

Row grouping (K dimension, 16 rows total):
Rows 0-3   map to register B0 for all threads
Rows 4-7   map to register B1 for all threads
Rows 8-11  map to register B2 for all threads
Rows 12-15 map to register B3 for all threads

Each thread holds 4 register fragments: B0, B1, B2, B3.

6. REGISTER PACKING
Data type is .f64 (64-bit). In PTX, f64 values require 64 bits of storage.
Each fragment Bn corresponds to one f64 element, stored in a 64-bit register (or equivalently two 32-bit registers as a register pair in PTX notation).
There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike f16 where two values share one 32-bit register). Each f64 element occupies its own 64-bit register fragment.
Each thread holds exactly 4 f64 register fragments: B0, B1, B2, B3, covering one column of matrix B across all 16 K-rows.

SUMMARY PER THREAD:
Every thread Tx (where x = 0 to 31) holds:
  B0: element at matrix B[row_group_0][col_x] where col_x = floor(x/4), row within group = x mod 4, rows 0-3
  B1: element at matrix B[row_group_1][col_x], rows 4-7
  B2: element at matrix B[row_group_2][col_x], rows 8-11
  B3: element at matrix B[row_group_3][col_x], rows 12-15

The label at the bottom "%laneid:{fragments}" confirms that the lane ID (thread index within warp, 0-31) determines which column and which row-within-group each thread owns.

* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.f64` | A vector expression containing four `.f64` registers containing `.f64` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f32` | A vector expression containing four `.f32` registers containing four `.f32` elements from the matrix C (or D). |
  | `.f16` | A vector expression containing two `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` elements from the matrix C (or D). |

   Dimensions: M=16 rows, N=8 columns, K=16

   Note: The label at the bottom reads "%laneid:{fragments}"

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled 0-15 (matrix rows) and columns labeled 0-7 (matrix columns).
   The columns are paired: columns 0-1 form one cell, columns 2-3 form one cell, columns 4-5 form one cell, columns 6-7 form one cell.
   There are 32 threads total (T0 through T31).

3. ELEMENT DISTRIBUTION BY THREAD AND REGISTER

   UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 0-7), registers c0 and c1:

   Row 0,  cols 0-1: T0  holds {c0, c1}
   Row 0,  cols 2-3: T1  holds {c0, c1}
   Row 0,  cols 4-5: T2  holds {c0, c1}

   Row 1,  cols 0-1: T4  holds {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  cols 2-3: T5  holds {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  cols 4-5: T6  holds {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  cols 6-7: T7  holds {c0, c1}

   Rows 2-6 follow the same pattern (indicated by arrows and ".." notation):
   Each row uses threads T(row*4+0) through T(row*4+3) for cols 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, all holding {c0, c1}.

   Row 7,  cols 0-1: T28 holds {c0, c1}
   Row 7,  cols 2-3: T29 holds {c0, c1}
   Row 7,  cols 4-5: T30 holds {c0, c1}

   Row 8,  cols 2-3: T1  holds {c2, c3}
   Row 8,  cols 4-5: T2  holds {c2, c3}
   Row 8,  cols 6-7: T3  holds {c2, c3}

   Row 9,  cols 0-1: T4  holds {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  cols 2-3: T5  holds {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  cols 4-5: T6  holds {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  cols 6-7: T7  holds {c2, c3}

   Rows 10-14 follow the same pattern (indicated by arrows and ".." notation):
   Each row uses threads T((row-8)*4+0) through T((row-8)*4+3) for cols 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, all holding {c2, c3}.

   Row 15, cols 0-1: T28 holds {c2, c3}
   Row 15, cols 2-3: T29 holds {c2, c3}
   Row 15, cols 4-5: T30 holds {c2, c3}
   Row 15, cols 6-7: T31 holds {c2, c3}

4. GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
   Threads T0-T3   cover all 8 columns of rows 0 and 8  (one thread per 2-column pair)
   Threads T4-T7   cover all 8 columns of rows 1 and 9
   Threads T8-T11  cover all 8 columns of rows 2 and 10
   Threads T12-T15 cover all 8 columns of rows 3 and 11
   Threads T16-T19 cover all 8 columns of rows 4 and 12
   Threads T20-T23 cover all 8 columns of rows 5 and 13
   Threads T24-T27 cover all 8 columns of rows 6 and 14
   Threads T28-T31 cover all 8 columns of rows 7 and 15

   General formula: Thread T(r*4 + c_pair) owns column pair c_pair*2 and c_pair*2+1 of row r (for r in 0-7) and row r+8 (for r in 0-7).

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds 4 register elements total: c0, c1, c2, c3.
   c0 and c1 are packed together (one 32-bit register holding two f16 values) covering the upper 8 rows (rows 0-7).
   c2 and c3 are packed together (one 32-bit register holding two f16 values) covering the lower 8 rows (rows 8-15).
   Total per thread: 2 registers (each 32-bit), holding 4 f16 accumulator values.
   Each thread covers exactly 2 columns (a 2-column-wide, 2-row-tall block: one row in the top half and one row in the bottom half).

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                               for ci where i <  2
           groupID + 8                             for ci where i >= 2

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)        for ci where i = {0,..,3}
  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.9. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k16` with integer type](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16816-i8-f8)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k16` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k16`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.u8` / `.s8` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing four `.u8` / `.s8` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7 |
  | `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing four `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 84](#mma-16816-a-i8).

  



[Figure: mma-16816-A-i8]
MMA .m16n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .u8/.s8 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
Operation: MMA .m16n8k16
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Data type: .u8 (unsigned 8-bit integer) or .s8 (signed 8-bit integer)
M=16, N=8, K=16

2. GRID STRUCTURE
Rows (R): 0 through 15 — these represent matrix rows
Columns (C): 0 through 15 — these represent K-dimension columns
The table is labeled R\C with row indices 0-15 and column indices 0-15
The columns are grouped into four bands: [0-3], [4-7], [8-11], [12-15]
The rows are grouped into two bands: [0-7] (upper half) and [8-15] (lower half)
Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (Rows 0-7, Columns 0-15):

Row 0, Columns 0-3:   T0:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 0, Columns 4-7:   T1:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 0, Columns 8-11:  T2:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 0, Columns 12-15: T3:{a0, a1, a2, a3}

Row 1, Columns 0-3:   T4:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 1, Columns 4-7:   T5:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 1, Columns 8-11:  T6:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 1, Columns 12-15: T7:{a0, a1, a2, a3}

Rows 2-6: Pattern continues (indicated by arrows showing continuation), threads T8-T27 follow the same pattern with {a0, a1, a2, a3}

Row 7, Columns 0-3:   T28:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 7, Columns 4-7:   T29:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 7, Columns 8-11:  T30:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 7, Columns 12-15: T31:{a0, a1, a2, a3}

LOWER HALF OF MATRIX (Rows 8-15, Columns 0-15):

Row 8,  Columns 0-3:   T0:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 8,  Columns 4-7:   T1:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 8,  Columns 8-11:  T2:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 8,  Columns 12-15: T3:{a4, a5, a6, a7}

Row 9,  Columns 0-3:   T4:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 9,  Columns 4-7:   T5:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 9,  Columns 8-11:  T6:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 9,  Columns 12-15: T7:{a4, a5, a6, a7}

Rows 10-14: Pattern continues (indicated by arrows), threads T8-T27 follow the same pattern with {a4, a5, a6, a7}

Row 15, Columns 0-3:   T28:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 15, Columns 4-7:   T29:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 15, Columns 8-11:  T30:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 15, Columns 12-15: T31:{a4, a5, a6, a7}

4. GROUPING PATTERNS
Each thread T0-T31 covers one 4-column group within one row.
Threads T0-T3 cover all 16 columns of row 0 (one thread per 4-column group).
Threads T4-T7 cover all 16 columns of row 1.
Each subsequent group of 4 threads covers the next row.
Threads T28-T31 cover all 16 columns of row 7.
The same threads T0-T31 are reused for rows 8-15, but hold elements a4-a7 instead of a0-a3.

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
Each thread holds 8 total elements split across two register groups:
  - Elements a0, a1, a2, a3: used for the upper 8 rows (rows 0-7)
  - Elements a4, a5, a6, a7: used for the lower 8 rows (rows 8-15)
Since the data type is 8-bit (.u8/.s8), four 8-bit values (a0-a3 or a4-a7) pack into one 32-bit register (4 bytes per register).
Thus each thread uses 2 registers total: one register holding {a0,a1,a2,a3} and one register holding {a4,a5,a6,a7}.
Each register contains 4 packed 8-bit integer values.

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
Operation: MMA .m16n8k16
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Data type: .u8 (unsigned 8-bit integer) or .s8 (signed 8-bit integer)
M=16, N=8, K=16

2. GRID STRUCTURE
Rows (R): 0 through 15 — these represent matrix rows
Columns (C): 0 through 15 — these represent K-dimension columns
The table is labeled R\C with row indices 0-15 and column indices 0-15
The columns are grouped into four bands: [0-3], [4-7], [8-11], [12-15]
The rows are grouped into two bands: [0-7] (upper half) and [8-15] (lower half)
Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (Rows 0-7, Columns 0-15):

Row 0, Columns 0-3:   T0:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 0, Columns 4-7:   T1:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 0, Columns 8-11:  T2:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 0, Columns 12-15: T3:{a0, a1, a2, a3}

Row 1, Columns 0-3:   T4:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 1, Columns 4-7:   T5:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 1, Columns 8-11:  T6:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 1, Columns 12-15: T7:{a0, a1, a2, a3}

Rows 2-6: Pattern continues (indicated by arrows showing continuation), threads T8-T27 follow the same pattern with {a0, a1, a2, a3}

Row 7, Columns 0-3:   T28:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 7, Columns 4-7:   T29:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 7, Columns 8-11:  T30:{a0, a1, a2, a3}
Row 7, Columns 12-15: T31:{a0, a1, a2, a3}

LOWER HALF OF MATRIX (Rows 8-15, Columns 0-15):

Row 8,  Columns 0-3:   T0:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 8,  Columns 4-7:   T1:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 8,  Columns 8-11:  T2:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 8,  Columns 12-15: T3:{a4, a5, a6, a7}

Row 9,  Columns 0-3:   T4:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 9,  Columns 4-7:   T5:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 9,  Columns 8-11:  T6:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 9,  Columns 12-15: T7:{a4, a5, a6, a7}

Rows 10-14: Pattern continues (indicated by arrows), threads T8-T27 follow the same pattern with {a4, a5, a6, a7}

Row 15, Columns 0-3:   T28:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 15, Columns 4-7:   T29:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 15, Columns 8-11:  T30:{a4, a5, a6, a7}
Row 15, Columns 12-15: T31:{a4, a5, a6, a7}

4. GROUPING PATTERNS
Each thread T0-T31 covers one 4-column group within one row.
Threads T0-T3 cover all 16 columns of row 0 (one thread per 4-column group).
Threads T4-T7 cover all 16 columns of row 1.
Each subsequent group of 4 threads covers the next row.
Threads T28-T31 cover all 16 columns of row 7.
The same threads T0-T31 are reused for rows 8-15, but hold elements a4-a7 instead of a0-a3.

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
Each thread holds 8 total elements split across two register groups:
  - Elements a0, a1, a2, a3: used for the upper 8 rows (rows 0-7)
  - Elements a4, a5, a6, a7: used for the lower 8 rows (rows 8-15)
Since the data type is 8-bit (.u8/.s8), four 8-bit values (a0-a3 or a4-a7) pack into one 32-bit register (4 bytes per register).
Thus each thread uses 2 registers total: one register holding {a0,a1,a2,a3} and one register holding {a4,a5,a6,a7}.
Each register contains 4 packed 8-bit integer values.


  Figure 84 MMA .m16n8k16 fragment layout for matrix A with `.u8` / `.s8` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                            for ai where i < 4
           groupID + 8                          for ai where i >= 4

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 4) + (i & 0x3)    for ai where i = {0,..,7}
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.u8` / `.s8` | A vector expression containing a single `.b32` register, containing four `.u8` / `.s8` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3 |
  | `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` | A vector expression containing a single `.b32` register, containing four `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1. b2. b3 |

##### 9.7.14.5.10. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k32`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16832)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k32` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k32`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  + `.s4` or `.u4` :

    > | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.u4` / `.s4` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a14, a15 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 87](#mma-16832-a-i4).
    >
    >

- Columns (horizontal axis): represent matrix column indices 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (N dimension, 8 columns total)

  Column 7: Thread T28, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD NUMBERING PATTERN
- Threads T0-T3 cover columns 0, rows 0-7 / 8-15 / 16-23 / 24-31 respectively
- Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, same row band pattern
- Each subsequent group of 4 threads covers the next column
- Thread Tx for column c, row band r: T = c*4 + r (where r = 0,1,2,3 for row bands 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31)
- Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
Each thread holds 8 elements labeled b0 through b7 within a single register (shown as a column vector).
Since the data type is 4-bit (.u4 or .s4), 8 elements of 4 bits each = 32 bits total, fitting exactly into one 32-bit register.
So each thread holds exactly 1 register containing 8 packed 4-bit values (b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7).

ELEMENT COVERAGE PER THREAD
Each thread covers 8 consecutive K-rows within its assigned row band and one specific N-column.
The 8 elements b0-b7 correspond to the 8 matrix rows within that thread's row band for its assigned column.

ARROW NOTATION
An arrow is drawn from the cell at row band 0-7 / column 2 area down to row band 24-31 / column 2 area, indicating the ".." columns follow the same pattern as the explicitly shown columns.

  Column 0: Thread T3, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T7, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T31, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD NUMBERING PATTERN
- Threads T0-T3 cover columns 0, rows 0-7 / 8-15 / 16-23 / 24-31 respectively
- Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, same row band pattern
- Each subsequent group of 4 threads covers the next column
- Thread Tx for column c, row band r: T = c*4 + r (where r = 0,1,2,3 for row bands 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31)
- Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
Each thread holds 8 elements labeled b0 through b7 within a single register (shown as a column vector).
Since the data type is 4-bit (.u4 or .s4), 8 elements of 4 bits each = 32 bits total, fitting exactly into one 32-bit register.
So each thread holds exactly 1 register containing 8 packed 4-bit values (b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7).

ELEMENT COVERAGE PER THREAD
Each thread covers 8 consecutive K-rows within its assigned row band and one specific N-column.
The 8 elements b0-b7 correspond to the 8 matrix rows within that thread's row band for its assigned column.

ARROW NOTATION
An arrow is drawn from the cell at row band 0-7 / column 2 area down to row band 24-31 / column 2 area, indicating the ".." columns follow the same pattern as the explicitly shown columns.

SUMMARY TABLE
Row Band  | Col 0 | Col 1 | Col 2 | ... | Col 7
0-7       |  T0   |  T4   |  T8   | ... |  T28
8-15      |  T1   |  T5   |  T9   | ... |  T29
16-23     |  T2   |  T6   |  T10  | ... |  T30
24-31     |  T3   |  T7   |  T11  | ... |  T31

Each thread holds 1 register with 8 packed 4-bit values (b0-b7), totaling 32 bits per register.

    >
    >
    > Figure 89 MMA .m16n8k32 fragment layout for matrix B with `.u4` / `.s4` type.
    >
    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =    (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7)      for bi where i = {0,..,7}
  col =     groupID
  ```

  + `.s8` or `.u8` or `.e4m3` or `.e5m2` or `.e3m2` or `.e2m3` or `.e2m1`:

    > | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.s8` / `.u8` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing four `.s8` / `.u8` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7 |
    > | `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing four `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 90](#mma-16832-b-i8-1) and
    > [Figure 91](#mma-16832-b-i8-2).

  Col 7: T28 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row Group 20-23:
  Col 0: T1  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T5  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T29 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row Group 24-27:

  Col 1: T6  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0-T3 cover row groups 16-19, 20-23, 24-27, 28-31 respectively at column 0
- Threads T4-T7 cover the same row groups at column 1

  where row_group_index = 0 for rows 16-19, 1 for 20-23, 2 for 24-27, 3 for 28-31
  and col ranges 0-7, giving thread numbers 0-31

ELEMENT NAMING AND PACKING:
- Each thread holds elements named b4, b5, b6, b7 (these are the SECOND set of 4 elements, continuing from b0-b3 in rows 0-15)
- For 8-bit types (.u8, .s8, .e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1): four 8-bit values are packed into registers

  | `.f16` | A vector expression containing two `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

Each thread holds 4 register elements total: c0, c1, c2, c3.

- Registers c0 and c1 cover the upper half of the matrix (rows 0-7) for that thread's assigned columns.
- Registers c2 and c3 cover the lower half of the matrix (rows 8-15) for that thread's assigned columns.
- Each thread entry shows {c0, c1} for rows 0-7 and {c2, c3} for rows 8-15, meaning two accumulator values are stored per thread per half.
- For .f16 type, two f16 values may be packed into a single 32-bit register.
- For .s32 and .f32 types, each element occupies one 32-bit register (c0, c1, c2, c3 are separate registers).

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY:

- 32 threads (T0-T31) cover the full 16x8 accumulator matrix.
- Each thread is responsible for exactly 2 columns and 2 rows (one in the upper half, one in the lower half), yielding 4 elements per thread total.
- Threads T0-T3 handle row 0; T4-T7 handle row 1; incrementing by 4 threads per row up to T28-T31 for row 7, then the pattern repeats for rows 8-15 with register indices c2,c3 instead of c0,c1.

 7  | T28: {c0, c1}   | T29: {c0, c1}   | T30: {c0, c1}   | T31: {c0, c1}
 8  | T0:  {c2, c3}   | T1:  {c2, c3}   | T2:  {c2, c3}   | T3:  {c2, c3}
 9  | T4:  {c2, c3}   | T5:  {c2, c3}   | T6:  {c2, c3}   | T7:  {c2, c3}
10  | (continuation arrow pattern — rows 10 through 14 follow same pattern)
... | ...             | ...             | ...             | ...
15  | T28: {c2, c3}   | T29: {c2, c3}   | T30: {c2, c3}   | T31: {c2, c3}

THREAD ASSIGNMENT DETAILS:

Upper half of matrix (rows 0-7):
- Row 0:  T0 (cols 0-1), T1 (cols 2-3), T2 (cols 4-5), T3 (cols 6-7)
- Row 1:  T4 (cols 0-1), T5 (cols 2-3), T6 (cols 4-5), T7 (cols 6-7)
- Row 2:  T8 (cols 0-1), T9 (cols 2-3), T10 (cols 4-5), T11 (cols 6-7)  [implied by arrow pattern]
- Row 3:  T12 (cols 0-1), T13 (cols 2-3), T14 (cols 4-5), T15 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 4:  T16 (cols 0-1), T17 (cols 2-3), T18 (cols 4-5), T19 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 5:  T20 (cols 0-1), T21 (cols 2-3), T22 (cols 4-5), T23 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 6:  T24 (cols 0-1), T25 (cols 2-3), T26 (cols 4-5), T27 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 7:  T28 (cols 0-1), T29 (cols 2-3), T30 (cols 4-5), T31 (cols 6-7)

Lower half of matrix (rows 8-15):
- Row 8:  T0 (cols 0-1), T1 (cols 2-3), T2 (cols 4-5), T3 (cols 6-7)
- Row 9:  T4 (cols 0-1), T5 (cols 2-3), T6 (cols 4-5), T7 (cols 6-7)
- Row 10: T8 (cols 0-1), T9 (cols 2-3), T10 (cols 4-5), T11 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 11: T12 (cols 0-1), T13 (cols 2-3), T14 (cols 4-5), T15 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 12: T16 (cols 0-1), T17 (cols 2-3), T18 (cols 4-5), T19 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 13: T20 (cols 0-1), T21 (cols 2-3), T22 (cols 4-5), T23 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 14: T24 (cols 0-1), T25 (cols 2-3), T26 (cols 4-5), T27 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 15: T28 (cols 0-1), T29 (cols 2-3), T30 (cols 4-5), T31 (cols 6-7)

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING:

Each thread holds 4 register elements total: c0, c1, c2, c3.

- Registers c0 and c1 cover the upper half of the matrix (rows 0-7) for that thread's assigned columns.
- Registers c2 and c3 cover the lower half of the matrix (rows 8-15) for that thread's assigned columns.
- Each thread entry shows {c0, c1} for rows 0-7 and {c2, c3} for rows 8-15, meaning two accumulator values are stored per thread per half.
- For .f16 type, two f16 values may be packed into a single 32-bit register.
- For .s32 and .f32 types, each element occupies one 32-bit register (c0, c1, c2, c3 are separate registers).

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY:

- 32 threads (T0-T31) cover the full 16x8 accumulator matrix.
- Each thread is responsible for exactly 2 columns and 2 rows (one in the upper half, one in the lower half), yielding 4 elements per thread total.
- Threads T0-T3 handle row 0; T4-T7 handle row 1; incrementing by 4 threads per row up to T28-T31 for row 7, then the pattern repeats for rows 8-15 with register indices c2,c3 instead of c0,c1.
- The arrows in rows 2 and 10 indicate the pattern continues (wrapping through T8-T27) between the explicitly shown rows.


  Figure 92 MMA .m16n8k32 fragment layout for accumulator matrix C/D with `.s32` / `.f32` / `.f16` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                           for ci where i <  2
           groupID + 8                         for ci where i >= 2

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci where i = {0,..,3}
  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.11. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k64`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16864)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k64` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k64`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.s4` / `.u4` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a30, a31 |
  | `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.e2m1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a30, a31 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 93](#mma-16864-a).

  Column 1: Thread T7 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, arrow from column 2 row 0-7 points down to column 2 row 24-31 indicating continuation)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T31 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN (column index to thread number):
  Column 0: T0 (rows 0-7), T1 (rows 8-15), T2 (rows 16-23), T3 (rows 24-31)
  Column 1: T4 (rows 0-7), T5 (rows 8-15), T6 (rows 16-23), T7 (rows 24-31)
  Column 2: (T8, T9, T10, T11 implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T28 (rows 0-7), T29 (rows 8-15), T30 (rows 16-23), T31 (rows 24-31)

General pattern: Thread number = (column_index * 4) + row_group_index
  where row_group_index = 0 for rows 0-7, 1 for rows 8-15, 2 for rows 16-23, 3 for rows 24-31

REGISTER PACKING:
Each thread holds 8 elements per register entry: b0, b1, b2 (implied by ".."), b3, b4, b5 (implied), b6, b7.
For .u4/.s4 types: 8 x 4-bit elements = 32 bits = one 32-bit register.
For .e2m1 type: 8 x 4-bit elements = 32 bits = one 32-bit register.
Elements b0 through b7 are packed sequentially within a single 32-bit register, with b0 in the least significant bits.

ARROW ANNOTATION:
A diagonal arrow in the figure spans from column 2 of row-group 0-7 down to column 2 of row-group 24-31, indicating that the pattern shown explicitly for columns 0, 1, and 7 continues uniformly across all 8 columns (0 through 7).

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7)          for bi where i < 8
             (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7) + 32     for bi where i >= 8

  col =   groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f32` | A vector expression containing four `.f32` registers, containing four `.f32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 96](#mma-16864-c).

##### 9.7.14.5.12. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k128`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-168128)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k128` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k128`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a62, a63 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 97](#mma-168128-a).

4. DATA TYPE
   .b1 (1-bit binary values). Since K=128 and each thread holds 32 elements (a0..a31 or a32..a63), and 32 bits fit in one 32-bit register, each register holds 32 packed 1-bit values. Thus each thread holds 2 registers total (one for a0..a31, one for a32..a63).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   - Threads T0..T31 are each assigned to exactly one (row, column-group) cell.
   - Rows 0..7 use matrix rows 0..7; threads T0..T31 are distributed 4 threads per row (one per column group), cycling T0-T3 for row 0, T4-T7 for row 1, ..., T28-T31 for row 7.
   - Rows 8..15 reuse the same threads T0..T31 in the same pattern, but with the second register set (a32..a63).
   - Each thread covers all 32 columns within its assigned column group (32 columns wide per group).

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   - Data type is .b1 (1-bit).
   - 32 consecutive 1-bit elements are packed into a single 32-bit register.
   - Each thread holds 2 registers: one register containing elements a0..a31 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values), and one register containing elements a32..a63 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values).
   - Total per thread: 64 bits (2 x 32-bit registers) covering 64 matrix elements across 128 columns (32 columns per column-group x 4 column-groups, but each thread is responsible for only one column-group of 32 columns, contributing 32 elements per register set).

   NOTE: The label at the bottom reads: %laneid:{fragments}, indicating the fragment assignment is indexed by lane ID within the warp.

   - Each thread holds 2 registers: one register containing elements a0..a31 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values), and one register containing elements a32..a63 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values).
   - Total per thread: 64 bits (2 x 32-bit registers) covering 64 matrix elements across 128 columns (32 columns per column-group x 4 column-groups, but each thread is responsible for only one column-group of 32 columns, contributing 32 elements per register set).

   NOTE: The label at the bottom reads: %laneid:{fragments}, indicating the fragment assignment is indexed by lane ID within the warp.


  Figure 97 MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for matrix A with `.b1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                              for ai where i < 32
            groupID + 8                           for ai where i >= 32

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 32) + (i & 0x1F)     for ai where i = {0, ...,63}
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing a single `.b32` register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, … , b30, b31 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 98](#mma-168128-b).

##### 9.7.14.5.13. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k256`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-168256)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k256` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k256`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a126, a127 |
